folio.label: 175v
folio.l:
For there nys no thyngö in this world æ he desiretú , saue oon-
ly worshipe & honour

¢ Fortæämore I knowe wel , & am rigút
sure , æ he shal no thyngö do in this nede , w outen my cou~-
seil ,

And I shal so werken in this cause , æ by the gce of oure
lord god , ©e shuln be reconsiled vn to vs

¢ Tha~ne seiden
they witú o vois ; worshipful lady , we putten vs , and
oure goodes , al fully in ©ourö wyl , & disposicio~n ,

& ben redy
to come what day æ it lyke , vn to ©oure noblesse , to lymy-
te vs , or assigne vs

for to make oure obligaco~ns and bon¶
as strongö as it liketú vn to ©oure goodnesse ,

æ we moun ful-
fille the wyl of ©ow , & of my lord Melibe

¢ Whan Dame Pè-
dence had herd the answeres of theise men , she bad hem go a
geyn péuely ,

and she retourned to hire lord Melibe , & told hi~
how she fond hise aduäsaries ful repentau~t ,

knowlechynge
ful lowely here sy~nes & trespas , and how they weren redy
to suffren al peyne ,

requerynge & preiynge hi~ , of mäcy &
pitee

¢ Tha~ne seide Melibe , he is wel worthy to haue ¹dou~
& for©efnesse of his synne , that excusetú nat his synne ,
but knowlechetú & repentetú hi~ , axynge indulgence ,,

¢ For Senek seitú ; there is the remissiou~ & for©efnesse , wherö
as the confessiou~ is ,

For Confession is neygúbore to Innocen-
ce ,
And æäfore I assente , and co~ferme me to haue pees ,

but it
is good , æ we do it nat witú outen thassent & wil , of oure
frendes
¢ Tha~ne was Pèdence rigút glad & ioyeful , & seide ,
Certes sire q¶ she , ©e han wel & goodly answered ;

For ri©t
as by the cou~seil , assent , & helpe of ©oure frendes , ©e han
be stired to venge ©ow , and make werre ,

Rigút so w outen
here cou~seil , shul ©e nat a corde ©ow , ne haue pees w ©ourö
aduäsaries ,

For the lawe seitú ; æere nys no thyngö so good ,
by weye of kynde , as a thyngö to ben vnbounde , by hi~ æ
it was I bounde

¢ And æa~ne Dame Pèdence w outen de-
lay or tariynge , sent a noon hire messageres for hire kyn
And for hire olde frendes , which æ were trewe and wyse

& told he~ by ordre in the päsence of Melibe , al this matere
as it is a boue expässed & declared ,

& preyed he~ , æ they wol-
de ©eue here auys & cou~seil , what were best to do in this
nede

¢ And whan Melibeus frendes , hadden taken here auys
& deliberaco~n of the forseid matere ,

& hadden examyned it ,
by gret bysynesse & gret diligence ;

they ©ouen ful cou~seil
for to haue pees & reste ,

& that Melibe shulde resseyue w
good herte hise aduäsaries , to for©ifnesse & mäcy

¢ And wha~
Dame Prudence had herd the assent of hire lord Melibe
& the cou~seil of hise frendes

a cord , w hire wyl & hire en-
tenco~n ;
she was wonderly glad in hire herte , and seide ,
